title: Planar Linear Arboricity Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: For any planar graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta \geq 5$, we have $\mathrm{la}(G) = \left\lceil \frac{\Delta}{2} \right\rceil$.
context_text: Cygan et al. proved $\mathrm{la}(G) = \lceil \Delta/2 \rceil$ for planar graphs with $\Delta \geq 9$ and posed this conjecture extending that result to all $\Delta \geq 5$. The conjecture implies the Vizing Planar Graph Conjecture, which is currently open only for $\Delta = 6$.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 2 (a refined partition result) for $\Delta = 9$, showing that edges of any planar graph with $\Delta(G) \leq 9$ can be partitioned into 4 linear forests and a matching; combined with Theorem 1, this yields $\mathrm{la}(G) = \lceil \Delta/2 \rceil$ for all planar $G$ with odd $\Delta \geq 9$ via a matching-augmented decomposition.
paper_title: Partitioning edges of a planar graph into linear forests and a matching

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: For every planar graph $G$ of odd maximum degree $\Delta \geq 7$ the edges of $G$ can be partitioned into $\frac{\Delta-1}{2}$ linear forests and one matching.
context_text: This conjecture refines the known linear arboricity bound for planar graphs with odd maximum degree by replacing one linear forest with the weaker structure of a matching. The case $\Delta \geq 11$ follows from existing results; the paper resolves $\Delta = 9$ (Theorem 2), leaving $\Delta = 7$ as the sole remaining open case.
paper_title: Partitioning edges of a planar graph into linear forests and a matching
